log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a UPX-compressed PE executable tagged as a 'spreader' with persistence capabilities. It's 1MB in size. I need to gather more IOC data to understand the full scope of the infection. Let m

The IOC summary confirms 14 dropped files, 1 registry persistence key, and 1 domain (VBOXSVR.ovh.net). The dropped files are clearly pornographic filenames masquerading as executables, which is a clas

Network activity shows only one DNS lookup to vboxsvr.ovh.net with no resolved IPs, suggesting either a failed resolution or the domain is used for something other than direct C2 (perhaps for download

The persistence mechanism is established via HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run, pointing to C:\Windows\mssrv.exe. This is a classic Run key persistence technique. I need to examine th

The dropped files confirm this is a dropper/spreader malware. It drops 14 files with pornographic filenames masquerading as executables (.exe, .mpg.exe, .rar.exe, .zip.exe) in various system directori
